title: 'Putin and Xi have an ace up their sleeve named Trump'
subhed: In his second term, the US president has confirmed his intention to distance the country from the 'Western collective.' Beijing and Moscow are both hoping to overturn the global order with Trump's help, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: France's Kouamé, 17, becomes youngest man into Slam third round since Nadal

Anti-Bolloré controversy could leave the entire French film industry worse off
subhed: The uproar between Canal+, the leading body funding French cinema, and the signatories of an open letter against Vincent Bolloré, Canal+'s owner, came at the worst possible time: in the middle of the Cannes Film Festival and as crucial agreements for the sector are up for negotiation.

title: In Brittany, luxury tourism is taking over the coastline
subhed: In Saint-Malo, a court granted approval at the end of March for the construction of a 4- and 5-star hotel complex and a thalassotherapy center on the site of a former municipal campground. This luxury project is one of many springing up across the region.

title: How much is your cat's life worth?
subhed: The 80 million pets in France are sometimes compared to overindulged children. Food, medical care, level of attachment: Is it reasonable to treat your pet like royalty?

title: By backtracking on environmental progress, Macron is 'betraying' the French, former members of the Climate Convention write
subhed: The signatories of this open letter warn about the government's dismantling of the Climate and Resilience law and call on citizens to defend 'their' law.

title: Kyiv attacks: The complex logistics network behind Russia's war machine
subhed: The Ukrainian capital endured a barrage of strikes over the weekend, including ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and long-range drones. These increasingly frequent hybrid operations reveal the intensification of Russian drone and missile production.

title: French banker lands exceptional and controversial contract to restructure Venezuelan debt
subhed: Matthieu Pigasse was tapped by Caracas to negotiate the restructuring of Venezuela's massive debt. His competitors suspect Washington played a role.
